chapter: ENG9_8 – A House Is Not a Home
title: A House Is Not a Home – Hard SAQs

questions.question: Explain why the author felt insecure in his new school.
questions.answer: He felt insecure because he had left his old friends and familiar environment behind.

questions.question: How did the author’s old teachers try to support him emotionally?
questions.answer: They encouraged him to participate in activities and make new friends.

questions.question: What role did the cat play in the author’s life?
questions.answer: The cat was a source of comfort and companionship for him.

questions.question: How did the fire affect the author emotionally?
questions.answer: It made him feel helpless, shocked, and deeply sad.

questions.question: Why did the author feel grateful to the firefighters?
questions.answer: They saved his mother and tried to comfort him during the crisis.

questions.question: Why was the loss of the cat especially painful for the author?
questions.answer: Because the cat was emotionally close to him.

questions.question: How did the tragedy change the author’s behaviour at school?
questions.answer: He became withdrawn and walked around without interest.

questions.question: Why did the author feel embarrassed by the attention he received?
questions.answer: He felt as if he was responsible for the incident.

questions.question: What was the significance of the gifts collected by classmates?
questions.answer: They showed kindness and support during a difficult time.

questions.question: How did the kindness of strangers influence the author’s thinking?
questions.answer: It made him realise that people can be generous and caring.

questions.question: Why did the author start making friends after the incident?
questions.answer: Because the kindness of his classmates made him open up.

questions.question: What symbolic meaning does the fire carry in the story?
questions.answer: It symbolises sudden tragedy and life’s challenges.

questions.question: How did the rebuilding of the house affect the author?
questions.answer: It gave him hope and a sense of a new beginning.

questions.question: Why did the woman who found the cat try to locate the owner?
questions.answer: She realised the cat was loved and missed.

questions.question: How did the author react when he got his cat back?
questions.answer: He hugged the cat tightly and cried with happiness.

questions.question: What does the cat symbolise in the story?
questions.answer: It symbolises love and emotional comfort.

questions.question: How did the author’s outlook toward life change?
questions.answer: He became more positive and appreciative of people.

questions.question: Why does the author say 'My cat was back and so was I'?
questions.answer: Because he felt emotionally restored and happy again.

questions.question: What does the story teach about human kindness?
questions.answer: Kindness can heal emotional wounds and restore hope.

questions.question: What message does the story convey about friendship?
questions.answer: True friendship appears in times of need.

questions.question: How does the story show the importance of empathy?
questions.answer: Through the help from classmates and the woman who returned the cat.

questions.question: Why is the title 'A House Is Not a Home' meaningful?
questions.answer: Because love and relationships make a place feel like home.

questions.question: What emotional journey does the author experience?
questions.answer: He moves from loneliness and loss to friendship and hope.

questions.question: Why is the story inspiring for readers?
questions.answer: It shows that kindness and support can overcome tragedy.

questions.question: What is the central message of the story?
questions.answer: Human kindness and relationships are more valuable than material possessions.
